perturbation.original_text: For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise, including but not limited to business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties . Confidential Information excludes, however, information which : (i) is or becomes known or available to Recipient without restriction from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient; (ii) is, or without violating the terms of this Agreement becomes , generally available to the public ; or (iii) is developed by Recipient independently of the information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise. Confidential Information excludes, however, information which : (i) is or becomes known or available to Recipient without restriction from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient; (ii) is, or without violating the terms of this Agreement becomes , generally available to the public ; or (iii) is developed by Recipient independently of the information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific examples of 'business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties' from the definition of 'Confidential Information,' the scope of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction because later sections rely on a clear understanding of this definition to enforce obligations. For example, Section 2a states Recipient shall not use the Confidential Information except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or, without the example being included, it creates uncertainty on whether these examples are part of the Confidential Information since they were initially included.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits specific examples of what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' making the definition more ambiguous than in the original text. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2a), which relies on a clear definition of 'Confidential Information' to restrict the Recipient's use and disclosure. Without the specific examples, it becomes unclear whether certain information falls under the definition, creating uncertainty and potential disputes about what the Recipient is obligated to protect. The perturbation explanation supports this, indicating the change creates ambiguity that impacts obligations in later sections.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'but in no event less than reasonable care', the contract lacks a minimum standard for protecting confidential information. This introduces an in-text contradiction. If Recipient's typical level of care for its own information is negligently low, this clause is insufficient; this could directly contradict Section 9: Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach. Because it does not ensure a minimum base of reasonable care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a minimum standard of 'reasonable care' for protecting confidential information. The changed text removes this minimum standard, potentially allowing Recipient to use a negligibly low standard of care if that is what they typically use for their own information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that money damages would not be sufficient for a breach and that Oceaneering is entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief. Without the 'reasonable care' baseline, the protection of confidential information is weakened, making the availability of equitable relief questionable and leading to a strong in-text contradiction, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Oceaneering grants no rights in or to the Confident ial Information . All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering.
perturbation.changed_text: Oceaneering grants no rights in or to the Confident ial Information .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering', it creates an uncertainty as to ownership of the confidential information. This could contradict Section 8, specifically (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion., which assumes Oceaneering's ownership of it when it refers to the possesion of it. The changed definition creates a scenario where rights to delete the information could be contested.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated that all Confidential Information remains the sole property of Oceaneering. The changed text removes this statement, creating ambiguity regarding ownership. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which assumes Oceaneering's ownership by stating that the recipient must deliver or destroy the Confidential Information at Oceaneering's request. Without the explicit statement of ownership, the recipient could argue they are not obligated to deliver or destroy the information, creating a clear and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any p rovision to the contrary notwithstanding, Recipient ’s obligations under this Agreement are subject to any disclosure requirement of law, regulation or legal process , but only to the extent of such requirement . Recipient shall promptly notify Oceaneering of any such requirement, cooperate fully with Oceaneering’s requests to prevent or minimize the effect of such disclosure , and make all reasonable efforts to have such disclosures placed under a protecti ve order or other wise obtain confidential treatment of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Any p rovision to the contrary notwithstanding, Recipient ’s obligations under this Agreement are subject to any disclosure requirement of law, regulation or legal process . Recipient shall promptly notify Oceaneering of any such requirement
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'but only to the extent of such requirement . Recipient shall promptly notify Oceaneering of any such requirement, cooperate fully with Oceaneering’s requests to prevent or minimize the effect of such disclosure , and make all reasonable efforts to have such disclosures placed under a protecti ve order or other wise obtain confidential treatment of the Confidential Information', it allows Recipient to disclose information beyond the requirement if they wish to. It creates a contradiction with Section 2, which states: 2. With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The original text created an obligation for the Recipient to protect confidential information. The changed text removes this obligation, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly states the Recipient 'shall not' disclose confidential information except in specific circumstances. The removal of the protective measures allows for disclosure beyond those circumstances, creating a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS DISCLOSED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATION , WARRANTY , ASSURANCE , GUARANTEE , OR INDUCEMENT OF ANY KIND , INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OF COMPLETENESS , ACCURACY , MERCHANTABILITY , SUITABILITY , NON-INFRINGEMENT OR FITN ESS FOR PURPOSE .
perturbation.changed_text: THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS DISCLOSED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATION , WARRANTY , ASSURANCE , GUARANTEE , OR INDUCEMENT OF ANY KIND .
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OF COMPLETENESS , ACCURACY , MERCHANTABILITY , SUITABILITY , NON-INFRINGEMENT OR FITN ESS FOR PURPOSE' leaves it open to interpretation whether those are implied. This contradicts with Section 3: Recipient shall require its Representatives who receive any Confidential Information to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and Recipient shall be responsible for their compliance herewith . Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care . since it is not ensured that the Confidental Information would be considered to be a material good and not a liability, which is implied when a warranty is assumed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall require its Representatives who receive any Confidential Information to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and Recipient shall be responsible for their compliance herewith . Recipient shall use at least the same degree of care to prote ct the confidentiality and ensure the proper use of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses with respect to its information of a similar kind or nature, but in no event less than reasonable care .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranty of completeness, accuracy, merchantability, suitability, non-infringement, or fitness for purpose. The changed text removes this explicit disclaimer, potentially implying that some warranties *may* apply. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted section which states that the recipient must protect the confidentiality of the information with at least the same degree of care as its own similar information, but no less than reasonable care. If the confidential information now has an implied warranty, the level of care required by the recipient is unclear, as they may be liable for issues the original text explicitly excluded. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agre ement sets forth the complete and exclusive statement of the parties’ agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be waived or modified except pursuant to a written agreement signed by the parties. Any waiver or forbearanc e on one occasion shall have no effect on any other occasion.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agre ement sets forth the complete and exclusive statement of the parties’ agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be waived or modified except pursuant to a written agreement signed by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Any waiver or forbearanc e on one occasion shall have no effect on any other occasion', we remove a clause that explicitly denies implied waivers. With this clause removed, Section 9's enforceability becomes uncertain: 'Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach'. Oceaneering's failure to seek injunctions initially could be interpreted as waiving the right to specific performance later.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, Oceaneering shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that forbearance on one occasion does not affect other occasions, preventing implied waivers. The changed text removes this clause. As per the perturbation explanation, this absence allows a scenario where Oceaneering's initial failure to seek injunctions (as stated in the Contradicted Text) could be interpreted as waiving their right to specific performance later, creating a contradiction between the enforceability of specific performance and the possibility of implied waiver due to the omission. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the purpose and enforceability of the contradicted text.
